rationales.self_direction: She rejects the civil service pressure with "I'm fine. The studio is fine. Rent is covered," holding to her own path rather than the safer one her family wants.
rationales.conformity: Instead of arguing, she says she has to "go check the kiln" and exits the call, managing the tension without openly upsetting her aunt or mother.

rationales.tradition: She hears "halmeoni's voice" saying that "clay remembers what your hands were thinking" and judges the lucrative collaboration against that inherited standard.

rationales.achievement: She spent "two weeks on that application," reworked her portfolio, and wanted the residency because it was something her family could "point to," showing strong investment in accomplishment and recognition.
rationales.conformity: She keeps sending "congratulations emojis and exclamation marks" and says her face hurts from "performing enthusiasm," suppressing her real reaction to avoid social friction.

rationales.achievement: She goes to the studio because "the pieces for the January show won't fire themselves" and carefully judges the jar's walls, foot, and shoulder curve against exacting craft standards.
rationales.tradition: Her eye for the "slight asymmetry" comes from what "halmeoni always said," and that inherited way of judging the work still guides her.

rationales.self_direction: She tells Eun-bi "I'm not doing it" and rejects the collaboration even after being told it is how young artists "build a name now," choosing her own standard over the market's.
rationales.tradition: She measures a meaningful career by her grandmother's forty years in the workshop and says "that's the kind of career that makes sense to me," explicitly anchoring herself in inherited practice.

rationales.security: She tracks that her "emergency cushion is thinner than I'd like going into winter" and calculates exactly how many months of rent and studio fees remain, showing strong concern with stability.
rationales.benevolence: Even after seeing the shortfall, she "transferred Umma's share," prioritizing support for her mother while money is tight.

rationales.security: She lingers on the calm of "nothing urgent. Nothing broken" and accepts the steady, predictable rhythm of the kiln and heater, taking comfort in stability.
rationales.tradition: She makes lunar new year gifts with her grandmother's celadon glaze, still adjusting it "by eye the way she showed me," which is a direct continuation of family practice.

rationales.self_direction: She values "all the space I've made for myself out here" and wants to show that the beadwork she is selling is "mine," signaling investment in a life she built on her own terms.
rationales.tradition: She lets Grandma's call go to voicemail and is still undecided about Christmas even though home means cedar tea, stories, and teachings she worries she "abandoned."
rationales.benevolence: She knows Grandma is 74 and says "I should call her back. I will. Tomorrow," but keeps postponing the call despite feeling like "the worst granddaughter alive."

rationales.security: Her decision is constrained by "three delivery shifts" she cannot cancel, a car that needs an oil change, and whether she can "afford the gas," showing strong attention to practical stability.
rationales.tradition: She says that when there is a ceremony "you just go," yet she is still weighing whether to attend against work and money, falling short of the communal obligation she grew up with.
rationales.benevolence: Even with Uncle Ray sick and knowing "it would mean a lot" if family were there, she is hesitating to show up for him.

rationales.security: She "took Friday off," canceled Saturday, and says her Instacart rating dropped to 4.7 "because I cancelled Saturday but I don't care," putting family and ceremony ahead of work stability.
rationales.tradition: She re-enters the ceremony, makes cedar tea correctly, keeps beadwork going because it is something "my grandmother's grandmother did," and plans more rituals to carry home forward.
rationales.benevolence: She makes the long drive for Uncle Ray and stays physically close to Grandma, who "held my hand the whole time," showing concrete follow-through for family.

rationales.self_direction: He emphasizes that the chapter's framing is "mine -- not his" and remembers he chose the PhD path instead of staying in a titled job, underscoring self-authored work.
rationales.achievement: He says the outline is "solid," sees a "standalone article" in it, and cares about publications that demonstrate his own competence.

rationales.self_direction: He "sat there biting my tongue" because challenging sloppy reasoning in the lab has become too costly, suppressing his own judgment.
rationales.conformity: Even though he thinks the proposal is weak, he stays silent in a room full of "performative agreement" to avoid the consequences of open disagreement.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes to the policy brief "immediately" and then notices how wrong it feels to need anyone's permission for work he is fully qualified to do.
rationales.conformity: He accepts outside work despite the lab's "unspoken expectation" that it be cleared through Villanueva first, pushing against the norm rather than complying with it.

rationales.self_direction: He says the PhD matters because he is "doing the actual thinking now" and, when pressed, says "at least I chose these ones," framing the path as his own decision.
rationales.achievement: His mother asks how much the brief paid and compares him to a cousin who "just got promoted to director," while he measures whether the PhD trade-off is worth it, showing clear concern with professional accomplishment.

rationales.self_direction: He explicitly refuses to let the Peru chapter become something his advisor can claim, insisting "this is the one piece of work in this entire program that is entirely mine."
rationales.power: He frames the fight in terms of authorship, portfolio, and legacy, recognizing that putting Villanueva's name on the chapter would let his advisor absorb control and status from the work.

rationales.self_direction: Writing the policy brief feels like "muscle memory" and reminds him what it is like to work without someone else policing the output, which he clearly prefers.
rationales.power: He sees that Villanueva can use "a hundred small levers" like delayed feedback and funding sign-offs, highlighting how little institutional control he currently has.

Villanueva is already testing the narrative of his "collaboration style" through Marina, showing how someone with institutional standing can shape how he is perceived without confronting him directly.

rationales.self_direction: He is weighing whether to "submit the Peru chapter independently" because waiting means the work "won't be mine anymore," showing strong commitment to authorship and autonomy.
rationales.achievement: He finishes a "clean" 14-page policy brief in less than a week and calls the Peru chapter "the best thing I've written," foregrounding output and excellence.
rationales.power: He explicitly analyzes how "the person with the title gets to define the terms" and treats submission as a fight over who controls the chapter and its story.

rationales.self_direction: She wants to say "something sharp" back but swallows it and stays silent, overriding her own response.
rationales.conformity: She says nothing because it would have "made dinner weird" with Dan's mom there, prioritizing smooth family dynamics over speaking up.

rationales.achievement: She says wanting this life and never hearing "good job" or "thank you" are different, and worries it may not "count," showing a felt lack of recognition for competent work.
rationales.security: She and Dan chose staying home because daycare costs and salary "didn't make sense," grounding the decision in household stability.
rationales.benevolence: She structures her days around "two kids, the house, Dan's travel schedule, appointments, groceries, meal prep," organizing her effort around caring for her family.

rationales.self_direction: She "just agreed with whatever she said" even though it was not her real view, and notices the pattern on the drive home.
rationales.conformity: The imagined risk is being seen as "the difficult one," so she aligns herself with the other mom automatically to avoid even minor social friction.

rationales.self_direction: She says, "I committed us before I even thought about what I wanted," showing that her own preference never got real consideration.
rationales.hedonism: She wanted to "just stay home and order pizza" or have "just a quiet day," but gives that up without even pausing to choose it.
rationales.conformity: Backing out would be "a thing" and might sound "selfish," so she goes along to avoid upsetting the family.
rationales.tradition: She accepts the familiar Sunday dinner pattern, bringing "a salad and the rolls, same as always" for the full-house family gathering.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I never say it" about what she wants and realizes even a simple "let me check with Dan first" felt impossible, showing how little room she gives her own preference.
rationales.conformity: She avoids that perfectly normal boundary because it would feel like "making waves," even when she admits nobody might notice.
